name: ZAYTUNA COLLEGE
mission: Zaytuna College aims to educate and prepare morally committed professional, intellectual, and spiritual leaders, who are grounded in the Islamic scholar...
programs: BA in Liberal and Islamic Studies
ZCELL (Center for Ethical Living and Learning)
theoryOfChange: Zaytuna College measures student achievement and success through indicators of their cumulative learning and growth, including writing and reasoning abilities, love of seeking knowledge, and commitment to the commonwealth. The learning outcomes of their degree programs define curricular success, supported by course learning outcomes. They consider their program successful if graduates find career success and are imbued with a lifelong love of learning and its responsibilities. The purpose of education, from an Islamic perspective and for Zaytuna College, is to aid students in their pursuit and discovery of truth, rooted in an Islamic epistemological framework.
scoreSummary: ZAYTUNA COLLEGE shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Western Association of Schools and Colleges Senior College and University Commission (WSCUC)
Accreditation (Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC))

zakatClaimEvidence: According to many scholars, institutions such as Zaytuna College meet the requirements for zakat eligibility. However, we have chosen to limit the zakat funds only to the total cost of education for zakat eligible students and not for any other institutional expenditures. We take this sacred trust very seriously, so if you choose to give a portion of your zakat to Zaytuna College, we will do our utmost, God willing, to ensure its most judicious and shariah compliant use. Zaytuna College applies zakat in five of the eight categories of recipients: (1) the poor (al-fuqarāʾ), (2) the needy (al-masākīn), (3) those in debt (al-ghārimūn), (4) the zakat collector, and (5) those who are in the way of Allah. The Zakat Fund serves as the basis of our financial aid program and is utilized to cover the total cost of educating a zakat-eligible student.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 34 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 3.0% of revenue ($533,781 on $18,021,649)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

keyConcerns.headline: Fundraising costs are 28% of total expenses
keyConcerns.detail: High fundraising spend reduces funds available for programs.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

dimension_explanations.alignment: The institution serves a specific niche in global education, focusing on the intersection of Islamic tradition and Western liberal arts [4]. It is verified as ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE, specifically for its scholarship funds which support eligible students [6][7]. The alignment score of 29/50 indicates a medium donor fit, as the mission is highly specialized toward academic and spiritual leadership [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: Zaytuna College provides direct educational services through accredited degree programs and intensive Arabic language courses [4][5]. While the college maintains a high data confidence level and significant digital reach, its impact score of 24/50 reflects a program expense ratio of 56.0%, suggesting a portion of the $18,021,649 total revenue is directed toward administrative and development costs necessary for institutional growth [1][2][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Zaytuna College is the first accredited Muslim liberal arts college in the United States, dedicated to grounding students in both Islamic scholarly traditions and the liberal arts.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.12 per $1 raised
Robust liquidity with 33.6 months of working capital available
Established zakat-eligibility policy for student scholarship support
baseline_narrative.summary: Located in Berkeley, California, Zaytuna College offers specialized degree programs including a Bachelor of Arts in Liberal Arts and Islamic Studies and a Master of Arts in Islamic Texts [4]. The institution operates with a total revenue of $18,021,649 and maintains a strong financial position with 33.6 months of working capital [1][2].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value the establishment of permanent Islamic intellectual institutions in the West and are comfortable with an endowment-building financial model.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The college provides verified graduation data and maintains rigorous <cite id="4">WASC accreditation</cite>, though long-term career outcome data is limited.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Zaytuna aims to produce leaders grounded in both Islamic and Western traditions to serve as a bridge between cultures and provide moral leadership.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Accreditation ensures that the degrees awarded have recognized value in the professional and academic marketplace.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the college has high reserves, its <cite id="2">19.7% revenue growth</cite> suggests it is successfully scaling its campus and faculty.

strengths.detail: The college maintains <cite id="1">33.6 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer against economic volatility.
Zaytuna operates with a lean development model, spending just <cite id="1">$0.12 per $1 raised</cite>.
The institution is <cite id="4">accredited by the WASC Senior College and University Commission</cite>, validating its dual-tradition curriculum.

strengths_deep_dive: Zaytuna College's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and financial resilience. With <cite id="2">total assets exceeding $50 million</cite> and a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 19.7%</cite>, the college has transitioned from a startup to an established institution. Its <cite id="6">zakat policy</cite> is robust, identifying five eligible categories including <cite id="6">fuqara and masakin</cite> to ensure religious compliance in scholarship distribution. Furthermore, its digital reach is significant, boasting <cite id="3">36 million digital views</cite>, which extends its educational impact far beyond its physical campus in Berkeley.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2009, Zaytuna College stands as the <cite id="4">first accredited Muslim liberal arts college</cite> in the United States, aiming to bridge Islamic scholarly traditions with the Western liberal arts. Based in Berkeley, California, the institution reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $18,359,667</cite> in 2023, primarily driven by <cite id="2">$15.2 million in contributions</cite>. The college offers a <cite id="4">BA in Liberal Arts and Islamic Studies</cite> and an <cite id="5">MA in Islamic Texts</cite>, focusing on preparing morally committed leaders. While the institution maintains <cite id="1">33.6 months of working capital</cite>, indicating exceptional financial stability, its <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 56.0%</cite> is notably lower than the <cite id="1">peer median of 85.3%</cite>. Despite this, Zaytuna demonstrates high fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="1">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>. The college has successfully <cite id="3">awarded 158 degrees</cite> to date and maintains <cite id="4">accreditation from the WASC Senior College and University Commission</cite>, ensuring its academic programs meet rigorous standards. For Muslim donors, the college provides a <cite id="6">verified zakat eligibility policy</cite>, specifically directing these funds toward student scholarships for those in need.
